design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate horizontally scrolling levels while managing 8-directional movement, offensive fire, and defensive blocking mechanics. Absorb power-ups and enemy fire while progressing through increasingly hostile biomes.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing aggressive offense (constant shooting) against defensive necessity (blocking projectiles with Power Star mechanic). Extremely fast scrolling and enemy movement creates time-pressure decision-making.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot an organic bioship as a scout for post-apocalyptic planetary reclamation; harness biological weaponry and adaptive tactics to survive hostile alien ecosystems and establish humanity's new home.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The Power Star blocking mechanic is unconventional for shmups—forcing players to position a defensive object rather than simply dodging. Four distinct organic ships with different flight speeds and weapon profiles demand strategic selection. The game refuses to let environmental scenery harm the player, focusing all danger on enemies and edge-crushing, creating clarity of threat.

difficulty.lives_system: Selectable at start: 1 to 5 lives (default 3). Extra lives earned via 1-UP icons or point milestones (20,000, 50,000, 100,000 points).
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects initial life count; difficulty gates content (last three of eight levels only available on higher difficulties). Fast scrolling and enemy patterns create execution-based challenge rather than pattern memorization.

progression.upgrade_system: Four selectable organic ships (Biowarriors) with varying flight speeds and distinct weapon arrays. Weapons differ per craft; no mid-run upgrades documented.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Difficulty selection gates final three levels (5-8). Progression is stage-by-stage linear with escalating enemy density and speed.
replayability_hooks: Four distinct ships with different handling and loadouts encourage replays with alternative vehicles
Selectable difficulty unlocks different stage counts
Leaderboard potential via point accumulation milestones

world_building_techniques: Opening exposition: planet Avaron biowar backstory and orbital Odysseus station setup
Organic ship design (Biowarriors) implies biological technology integration into vehicles
Level environments reflect hostile post-biowar planetary conditions
Closing sequence: scouts return to mothership, implying mission success and narrative closure

faction_indicators: Human survivors on O.P. Odysseus (remnant civilization seeking reclamation)
Hostile alien/biological entities on Avaron (implicit enemy faction)
environmental_storytelling: Deep, bass-heavy music reflects dark biowar atmosphere
Colorful but hostile alien landscapes signal uninviting biomes
Foreground/background layering suggests organic, infected environments
Progressive difficulty and stage design implies increasingly dangerous Avaron zones

sources_used: Wikipedia - Bio-Hazard Battle (gameplay, plot, reception, mechanics)
